examples: A train passenger traveling from Baghdad to Karbala, a Shiite holy city some 60 miles south of the capital, reads a prayer book.
The execution, carried out last month by Iraq's largest Shiite militia, would have been unexceptional but for one fact: The victim was one of its own.
Saudi Arabia has also been concerned by what it sees as Iranian meddling in Yemen in support of the rebellion by the Houthis (a Shiite minority in the north).
Iranians are predominately Shiites while Syria's ruling elite is most Alawite, an offshoot of Shiite Islam.
The problem in Iraq is that 60% of the population consists of Shiite Muslims.
